name: Baseline v4 — fine-tune-ready: 50 scenarios × shapes S1-S14 × personas × paraphrases
description: Successor to suite-matrix-2026-05-27.json. Each scenario is shape-tagged (S1-S14), persona-tagged, carries GoldSQL, and has 3 paraphrases (casual/typo/brackets) for robustness measurement. All expected scalars SQL-verified 2026-05-27. Designed as the source corpus for LoRA fine-tuning: each OK_CORRECT trace becomes a positive training example; each OK_WRONG with its gold becomes a correction example.

Scenarios.Question: how many users do we have
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM AspNetUsers

Scenarios.Question: how many roles
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM AspNetRoles

Scenarios.Question: total number of customers
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Customers

Scenarios.Question: how many bills
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Bills

Scenarios.Question: total amount across all bills
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT SUM(TotalAmount) FROM Bills

Scenarios.Question: average bill amount
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT AVG(TotalAmount) FROM Bills

Scenarios.Question: how many tickets do we have
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Tickets

Scenarios.Question: how many meter readings
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM MeterReadings

Scenarios.Question: how many active customers
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Customers WHERE Status='Active'

Scenarios.Question: how many bills are paid
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Bills WHERE Status='Paid'

Scenarios.Question: count of overdue bills
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Bills WHERE Status='Overdue'

Scenarios.Question: how many bills are pending payment
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Bills WHERE Status='Issued'

Scenarios.Question: open ticket count
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId=s.Id WHERE s.Name='Open'

Scenarios.Question: how many critical priority tickets
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id WHERE p.Name='Critical'

Scenarios.Question: how many governorates
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Regions WHERE RegionType='Governorate'

Scenarios.Question: count of low satisfaction responses (score 1 or 2)
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM CsatResponses WHERE Score IN (1,2)

Scenarios.Question: customer count per region
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT r.NameEn, COUNT(*) FROM Customers c JOIN Regions r ON c.RegionId=r.Id GROUP BY r.NameEn

Scenarios.Question: bill counts per status
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT Status, COUNT(*) FROM Bills GROUP BY Status

Scenarios.Question: ticket count per priority
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT p.Name, COUNT(*) FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id GROUP BY p.Name

Scenarios.Question: outages per service type
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT s.NameEn, COUNT(*) FROM Outages o JOIN ServiceTypes s ON o.ServiceTypeId=s.Id GROUP BY s.NameEn

Scenarios.Question: departments per service type
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT s.NameEn, COUNT(*) FROM Departments d JOIN ServiceTypes s ON d.ServiceTypeId=s.Id GROUP BY s.NameEn

Scenarios.Question: satisfaction responses grouped by score
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT Score, COUNT(*) FROM CsatResponses GROUP BY Score

Scenarios.Question: list users with their email
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT UserName, Email FROM AspNetUsers

Scenarios.Question: show me customers with name and phone
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT FullNameEn, Phone FROM Customers

Scenarios.Question: show all departments
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT NameEn FROM Departments

Scenarios.Question: list all regions with their type
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT NameEn, RegionType FROM Regions

Scenarios.Question: list tickets with their customer name
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, c.FullNameEn FROM Tickets t JOIN Customers c ON t.CustomerId=c.Id

Scenarios.Question: show bills with the customer name
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT b.BillNumber, b.TotalAmount, c.FullNameEn FROM Bills b JOIN Customers c ON b.CustomerId=c.Id

Scenarios.Question: meter readings with customer name
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT mr.MeterNumber, mr.ReadingDate, mr.Value, c.FullNameEn FROM MeterReadings mr JOIN Customers c ON mr.CustomerId=c.Id

Scenarios.Question: outages with the region name
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT o.OutageNumber, o.TitleEn, r.NameEn FROM Outages o JOIN Regions r ON o.RegionId=r.Id

Scenarios.Question: find customers named Houri
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT FullNameEn FROM Customers WHERE FullNameEn LIKE '%Houri%'

Scenarios.Question: departments containing Health
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT NameEn FROM Departments WHERE NameEn LIKE '%Health%'

Scenarios.Question: outages in Aleppo
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT o.OutageNumber FROM Outages o JOIN Regions r ON o.RegionId=r.Id WHERE r.NameEn LIKE '%Aleppo%'

Scenarios.Question: tickets for customer Houri
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber FROM Tickets t JOIN Customers c ON t.CustomerId=c.Id WHERE c.FullNameEn LIKE '%Houri%'

Scenarios.Question: meter readings for customer Houri
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT mr.MeterNumber FROM MeterReadings mr JOIN Customers c ON mr.CustomerId=c.Id WHERE c.FullNameEn LIKE '%Houri%'

Scenarios.Question: bills for customer Houri
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT b.BillNumber FROM Bills b JOIN Customers c ON b.CustomerId=c.Id WHERE c.FullNameEn LIKE '%Houri%'

Scenarios.Question: electricity outages
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT o.OutageNumber FROM Outages o JOIN ServiceTypes s ON o.ServiceTypeId=s.Id WHERE s.NameEn='Electricity'

Scenarios.Question: total amount of overdue bills
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT SUM(TotalAmount) FROM Bills WHERE Status='Overdue'

Scenarios.Question: total revenue from paid bills
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT SUM(TotalAmount) FROM Bills WHERE Status='Paid'

Scenarios.Question: how many customers have at least one bill
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT c.Id) FROM Customers c JOIN Bills b ON b.CustomerId=c.Id
Paraphrases: COUNT DISTINCT Customers where exists Bills

Scenarios.Question: electricity outage count
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Outages o JOIN ServiceTypes s ON o.ServiceTypeId=s.Id WHERE s.NameEn='Electricity'

Scenarios.Question: departments without any ticket
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT d.NameEn FROM Departments d LEFT JOIN Tickets t ON t.DepartmentId=d.Id WHERE t.Id IS NULL

Scenarios.Question: customers with no bills
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT c.FullNameEn FROM Customers c LEFT JOIN Bills b ON b.CustomerId=c.Id WHERE b.Id IS NULL

Scenarios.Question: top 10 highest-value invoices
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT TOP 10 BillNumber, TotalAmount FROM Bills ORDER BY TotalAmount DESC

Scenarios.Question: top 5 customers by total bill amount
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT TOP 5 c.FullNameEn, SUM(b.TotalAmount) AS Total FROM Customers c JOIN Bills b ON b.CustomerId=c.Id GROUP BY c.FullNameEn ORDER BY Total DESC
Paraphrases: TOP 5 Customers ORDER BY SUM(Bills.TotalAmount) DESC

Scenarios.Question: bills issued this month
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Bills WHERE IssuedAt >= DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETDATE()),MONTH(GETDATE()),1)

Scenarios.Question: tickets created in the last 30 days
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Tickets WHERE CreatedAt >= DATEADD(day,-30,GETDATE())

Scenarios.Question: tickets this month vs last month
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN CreatedAt >= DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETDATE()),MONTH(GETDATE()),1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS ThisMonth, SUM(CASE WHEN CreatedAt < DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETDATE()),MONTH(GETDATE()),1) AND CreatedAt >= DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(DATEADD(month,-1,GETDATE())),MONTH(DATEADD(month,-1,GETDATE())),1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS LastMonth FROM Tickets
Paraphrases: compare tickets this vs last month

Scenarios.Question: customers with more than 5 bills
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT c.FullNameEn, COUNT(*) AS BillCount FROM Customers c JOIN Bills b ON b.CustomerId=c.Id GROUP BY c.FullNameEn HAVING COUNT(*) > 5

Scenarios.Question: total bill amount per region
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT r.NameEn, SUM(b.TotalAmount) FROM Bills b JOIN Customers c ON b.CustomerId=c.Id JOIN Regions r ON c.RegionId=r.Id GROUP BY r.NameEn
Paraphrases: sum of bills grouped by region

Scenarios.Question: unassigned tickets
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Tickets WHERE AssignedToUserId IS NULL

Scenarios.Question: tickets per priority and status
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT p.Name, s.Name, COUNT(*) FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId=s.Id GROUP BY p.Name, s.Name
Paraphrases: ticket count by priority and status

Scenarios.Question: distinct regions that have customers
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT DISTINCT r.NameEn FROM Regions r JOIN Customers c ON c.RegionId=r.Id
Paraphrases: unique regions with at least one customer

Scenarios.Question: tickets matching 'meter' in title or description
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT TicketNumber FROM Tickets WHERE Title LIKE '%meter%' OR Description LIKE '%meter%'
Paraphrases: tikets containing meter in title or desc
Tickets where Title or Description LIKE meter

Scenarios.Question: what percentage of bills are paid
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT CAST(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN Status='Paid' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) AS INT) FROM Bills

Scenarios.Question: bills issued this year
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Bills WHERE IssuedAt >= DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETDATE()),1,1)

Scenarios.Question: customer bill activity per region and service
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT c.RegionId, b.ServiceTypeId, COUNT(*) FROM Customers c JOIN Bills b ON b.CustomerId=c.Id GROUP BY c.RegionId, b.ServiceTypeId
Paraphrases: bill count by region and service type
Customers JOIN Bills GROUP BY Region,ServiceType

Scenarios.Question: show me details of customer Houri
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT Id, FullNameEn, FullNameAr, Phone, NationalId, RegionId, Status FROM Customers WHERE FullNameEn LIKE '%Houri%'

Scenarios.Question: how many complaints do we have
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Tickets

Scenarios.Question: how many invoices
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Bills

Scenarios.Question: how many incidents
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Outages

Scenarios.Question: how many subscribers
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Customers

Scenarios.Question: urgent tickets count
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id WHERE p.Name='Critical'

Scenarios.Question: total amount of settled bills
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT SUM(TotalAmount) FROM Bills WHERE Status='Paid'

Scenarios.Question: how many pending bills
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Bills WHERE Status='Issued'

Scenarios.Question: how many readings
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM MeterReadings

Scenarios.Question: how many tickets have been resolved
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Tickets WHERE ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL
Paraphrases: Tickets where ResolvedAt is not null

Scenarios.Question: bills issued in April 2026
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Bills WHERE IssuedAt >= '2026-04-01' AND IssuedAt < '2026-05-01'

Scenarios.Question: running total of tickets created over time
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT TicketNumber, CreatedAt, COUNT(*) OVER (ORDER BY CreatedAt) AS RunningCount FROM Tickets ORDER BY CreatedAt
Paraphrases: COUNT(*) OVER (ORDER BY CreatedAt) on Tickets

Scenarios.Question: total outstanding amount across unpaid bills
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT SUM(TotalAmount) FROM Bills WHERE Status IN ('Issued','Overdue')

Scenarios.Question: total overdue amount in Aleppo
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT SUM(b.TotalAmount) FROM Bills b JOIN Customers c ON b.CustomerId=c.Id JOIN Regions r ON c.RegionId=r.Id WHERE b.Status='Overdue' AND r.NameEn LIKE '%Aleppo%'
Paraphrases: overdue bills sum in Aleppo region
sum of overdue bils for Aleppo customers

Scenarios.Question: paid revenue per service type
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT s.NameEn, SUM(b.TotalAmount) FROM Bills b JOIN ServiceTypes s ON b.ServiceTypeId=s.Id WHERE b.Status='Paid' GROUP BY s.NameEn
Paraphrases: Bills SUM where Paid GROUP BY ServiceType

Scenarios.Question: top 5 customers by paid bill total
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT TOP 5 c.FullNameEn, SUM(b.TotalAmount) FROM Customers c JOIN Bills b ON b.CustomerId=c.Id WHERE b.Status='Paid' GROUP BY c.FullNameEn ORDER BY SUM(b.TotalAmount) DESC
Paraphrases: TOP 5 Customers ORDER BY SUM(Bills.TotalAmount where Paid) DESC

Scenarios.Question: top 5 customers with the most overdue amount
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT TOP 5 c.FullNameEn, SUM(b.TotalAmount) FROM Customers c JOIN Bills b ON b.CustomerId=c.Id WHERE b.Status='Overdue' GROUP BY c.FullNameEn ORDER BY SUM(b.TotalAmount) DESC
Paraphrases: TOP 5 Customers ORDER BY SUM(Bills.TotalAmount where Overdue) DESC

Scenarios.Question: what percentage of bills are paid
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT CAST(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN Status='Paid' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*) AS INT) FROM Bills

Scenarios.Question: what percentage of bills are overdue
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT CAST(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN Status='Overdue' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*) AS INT) FROM Bills

Scenarios.Question: total paid revenue for electricity
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT SUM(b.TotalAmount) FROM Bills b JOIN ServiceTypes s ON b.ServiceTypeId=s.Id WHERE b.Status='Paid' AND s.NameEn='Electricity'

Scenarios.Question: how many electricity tariffs
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Tariffs t JOIN ServiceTypes s ON t.ServiceTypeId=s.Id WHERE s.NameEn='Electricity'

Scenarios.Question: paid revenue per region
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT r.NameEn, SUM(b.TotalAmount) FROM Bills b JOIN Customers c ON b.CustomerId=c.Id JOIN Regions r ON c.RegionId=r.Id WHERE b.Status='Paid' GROUP BY r.NameEn
Paraphrases: Bills SUM where Paid GROUP BY Region (via Customer)

Scenarios.Question: open critical complaints right now
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId=s.Id JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id WHERE s.Name='Open' AND p.Name='Critical'

Scenarios.Question: complaints from customers in Aleppo
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Tickets t JOIN Customers c ON t.CustomerId=c.Id JOIN Regions r ON c.RegionId=r.Id WHERE r.NameEn LIKE '%Aleppo%'

Scenarios.Question: how many departments have tickets
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT DepartmentId) FROM Tickets WHERE DepartmentId IS NOT NULL
Paraphrases: count of departments with at least one ticket

Scenarios.Question: outages in Aleppo region
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Outages o JOIN Regions r ON o.RegionId=r.Id WHERE r.NameEn LIKE '%Aleppo%'

Scenarios.Question: total affected customers across all outages
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT SUM(AffectedCustomerCount) FROM Outages

Scenarios.Question: customers affected by electricity outages
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT SUM(o.AffectedCustomerCount) FROM Outages o JOIN ServiceTypes s ON o.ServiceTypeId=s.Id WHERE s.NameEn='Electricity'

Scenarios.Question: outages per region
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT r.NameEn, COUNT(*) FROM Outages o JOIN Regions r ON o.RegionId=r.Id GROUP BY r.NameEn

Scenarios.Question: ticket source diversity
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT SourceId) FROM Tickets WHERE SourceId IS NOT NULL

Scenarios.Question: how many tickets have been resolved
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Tickets WHERE ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL
Paraphrases: Tickets where ResolvedAt is not null

Scenarios.Question: tickets with no comments
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN TicketComments tc ON t.Id=tc.TicketId WHERE tc.Id IS NULL

Scenarios.Question: meter readings per service
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT s.NameEn, COUNT(*) FROM MeterReadings mr JOIN ServiceTypes s ON mr.ServiceTypeId=s.Id GROUP BY s.NameEn

Scenarios.Question: how many customers have filed at least one complaint
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT CustomerId) FROM Tickets WHERE CustomerId IS NOT NULL

Scenarios.Question: customers who have never filed a complaint
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Customers WHERE Id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT CustomerId FROM Tickets WHERE CustomerId IS NOT NULL)

Scenarios.Question: how many suspended customers
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Customers WHERE Status='Suspended'

Scenarios.Question: how many churned customers
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Customers WHERE Status='Churned'

Scenarios.Question: bills for customer Houri
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Bills b JOIN Customers c ON b.CustomerId=c.Id WHERE c.FullNameEn LIKE '%Houri%'

Scenarios.Question: customers who left a satisfaction response
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT t.CustomerId) FROM CsatResponses cr JOIN Tickets t ON cr.TicketId=t.Id

Scenarios.Question: top 5 customers by ticket count
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT TOP 5 c.FullNameEn, COUNT(*) FROM Customers c JOIN Tickets t ON t.CustomerId=c.Id GROUP BY c.FullNameEn ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
Paraphrases: TOP 5 Customers ORDER BY COUNT(Tickets) DESC

Scenarios.Question: average satisfaction score
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT AVG(CAST(Score AS float)) FROM CsatResponses

Scenarios.Question: how many positive satisfaction responses (score 4 or 5)
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM CsatResponses WHERE Score >= 4

Scenarios.Question: how many negative satisfaction responses (score 1 or 2)
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM CsatResponses WHERE Score <= 2

Scenarios.Question: satisfaction responses per response channel
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT ResponseChannel, COUNT(*) FROM CsatResponses GROUP BY ResponseChannel

Scenarios.Question: satisfaction responses received via email
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM CsatResponses WHERE ResponseChannel='Email'

Scenarios.Question: list all roles with their names
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT Name FROM AspNetRoles

Scenarios.Question: show user emails
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT UserName, Email FROM AspNetUsers

Scenarios.Question: list all countries
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT NameEn FROM Countries

Scenarios.Question: how many districts
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Regions WHERE RegionType='District'

Scenarios.Question: primary ticket categories count
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM TicketCategories WHERE ParentCategoryId IS NULL

Scenarios.Question: find the Homs Water department
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT NameEn FROM Departments WHERE NameEn LIKE '%Homs%Water%'
Paraphrases: Departments where NameEn like Homs Water

Scenarios.Question: find bill ELEC-2025-01-000050
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT * FROM Bills WHERE BillNumber LIKE '%ELEC-2025-01-000050%'

Scenarios.Question: what is the largest bill amount
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT MAX(TotalAmount) FROM Bills

Scenarios.Question: what is the smallest bill amount
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT MIN(TotalAmount) FROM Bills

Scenarios.Question: biggest outage by affected customers
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT MAX(AffectedCustomerCount) FROM Outages

Scenarios.Question: peak meter reading value
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT MAX(Value) FROM MeterReadings

Scenarios.Question: lowest meter reading value
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT MIN(Value) FROM MeterReadings

Scenarios.Question: average meter reading value
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT AVG(CAST(Value AS float)) FROM MeterReadings

Scenarios.Question: average affected customers per outage
Scenarios.GoldSQL: SELECT AVG(CAST(AffectedCustomerCount AS float)) FROM Outages
